description_long: This object is a sleek, single-fuselage glider-style airplane with a long, narrow wingspan and a streamlined nose. The wings are straight and extend symmetrically from the midsection of the fuselage, with subtle blue tips at each end. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and features a transparent canopy, likely accommodating a single pilot. The tail section consists of a conventional horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin, both in a minimalist design. The surface appears smooth and metallic, with a light gray coloration dominating the body. No visible engines or complex landing gear are present, suggesting a lightweight, unpowered glider design.
description: A streamlined, light gray glider airplane with long, straight wings, a single transparent cockpit canopy, and a minimalist tail section.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, cockpit canopy, and tail configuration.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow fuselage, straight wings, and blue-tipped wingtips.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the underside of the wings and fuselage, with the tail and cockpit visible.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the horizontal stabilizer, vertical fin, and the symmetry of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with long, straight wings and slight dihedral; blue-tipped wingtips and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engine; appears to be an unpowered glider.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No visible landing gear; likely a skid or retractable single-wheel typical of gliders.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a transparent canopy.
